_metadata.description: Maps ACLED (Armed Conflict Location & Event Data) event_type and sub_event_type fields to the 8-category geopolitical event taxonomy. ACLED is the primary source for Armed Conflict & Instability and a secondary source for Political Transitions & Volatility. Its strength is precise location data (lat/lon) and fatality counts, which drive severity scoring. Validated against: Russia-Ukraine war (2022-present — dense Battle/Explosion entries for UA/RU), Sudan civil war (2023), Gaza conflict (2023-2024).

severity_scoring_from_fatalities.note: Fatality-based severity is a floor — apply max(fatality_severity, default_subtype_severity)

Battles.note: All battle events map directly to Armed Conflict. Severity driven by fatalities and actor type.

Armed clash.note: Skirmishes, limited engagements; severity upgraded if fatalities > 10

Government regains territory.note: Indicates active territorial conflict; track as ongoing event

Non-state actor overtakes territory.note: High-severity instability; significant business disruption risk in affected area

Explosions/Remote violence.note: Remote violence, IEDs, drone strikes, shelling — all armed conflict indicators.

Air/drone strike.note: State or non-state actor aerial attack; infrastructure disruption risk

Shelling/artillery/missile attack.note: Sustained fire; indicates active frontline or long-range targeting
Shelling/artillery/missile attack.validation_note: Ukraine 2022: dense shelling events should populate RU/UA with severity 4-5

Remote explosive/landmine/IED.note: Persistent security risk; relevant for route and logistics disruption assessment

Grenade.note: Lower severity unless in business-critical area

Chemical weapon.note: Always severity 5; typically triggers sanctions cascade

Sea mine/underwater IED.note: Maritime security — high logistics disruption risk; e.g., Red Sea mining

Missile attack.note: Ballistic or cruise missile strikes; infrastructure destruction risk

Violence against civilians.note: Targeting of civilian population — key indicator for forced displacement, infrastructure destruction, and corporate evacuation risk.

Abduction/forced disappearance.note: Relevant for workforce safety; indicates hostile actor presence

Sexual violence.note: Severe human rights indicator; relevant for ESG/reputation channel

Looting/property destruction.note: Direct asset destruction risk for operating companies

Protests.note: Most protests are background noise. Include only if: (a) anti-government and >1000 participants, or (b) targeting foreign companies/governments, or (c) leading to government response. Severity upgrades based on scale and government response.

Peaceful protest.note: Low signal; include only if large-scale (national) or economically targeted
Peaceful protest.filter: Include only if notes contain government, foreign company, or economic policy keywords

Protest with intervention.note: Government/police intervention indicates elevated political tension

Excessive force against protesters.note: Significant regime instability signal; also ESG/reputation risk

Riots.note: Riots indicate breakdown of public order. Cross-classify as Armed Conflict if fatalities > 10 or if triggered by political event (election, coup).

Mob violence.note: Inter-group violence; upgrade to armed_conflict_instability if ethnic/sectarian

Strategic developments.note: Catch-all ACLED category — requires sub_event_type filtering to be useful. Most map to political_transitions_volatility or institutional_alliance_realignment.

Headquarters or base established.note: Non-state actor establishing presence; territory control signal

Looting/property seized.note: Asset seizure — direct financial impact on operators

Disruption of weapons use.note: De-escalation signal; track as conflict end marker

Agreement.note: Peace agreements, ceasefires; positive instability resolution

Coup.note: Regime change by force — maximum political instability; always severity 5
Coup.validation_note: Sudan coup (Oct 2021), Niger coup (Jul 2023) should appear as severity 5

Government officials arrested.note: Purge indicator; high policy reversal risk

Non-violent transfer of territory.note: Political settlement; monitor for compliance/regulatory implications

Peaceful transfer of power.note: Normal election outcome; include if political platform has significant business implications

acled_deduplication_notes.spatial_clustering: ACLED events in the same country on the same date with the same event_type can represent multiple reports of one event. Group by (country, event_date, event_type, actor1) within 50km radius for deduplication.
acled_deduplication_notes.temporal_clustering: For ongoing conflicts (e.g., Ukraine), daily battle events are separate events, not duplicates — do not over-deduplicate.
acled_deduplication_notes.fatality_aggregation: When grouping events for database storage, sum fatalities across deduplicated cluster, not just take max.
